text.title: agentic-rag-self-correction Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for agentic-rag-self-correction. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: The faithfulness verifier MUST use a different model (or at minimum a different temperature/run) from the generator. When faithfulness_verifier returns more than 2 ungrounded claims, do NOT surface the answer to the user — either retrieve more context or escalate to human review.
rule.rationale: The same model tends to confirm its own outputs — a generator that hallucinated will call its own answer faithful. Using a different model (or temperature) for verification breaks this confirmation bias and catches the hallucinations the generator missed.

rule.statement: Cap self-correction at max_corrections (recommended 2). When the cap is reached without a verified answer, log all iteration traces and route to human review.
rule.rationale: Self-correction loops can amplify hallucinations if the verification LLM uses the same model as the generator. A hard cap prevents runaway cost and surfaces persistent failures for corpus review.
